customers.name: Global Frequent Flyer Member
customers.description: A high-value long-haul traveller who expects premium service, loyalty recognition, fast self-service, and confidence that disruptions will be handled without excessive effort.
careAbout: Finding the right premium itinerary without price or fare-rule confusion
Recognised identity, tier status, points, and upgrade options across channels
Fast, proactive communication before and during travel
Reliable disruption handling when delays or cancellations happen
A joined-up journey across airline, alliance, and airport moments
winsThem: Personalised offers and upgrades that respect status and trip context
One account that ties booking, loyalty, documents, seats, and service together
Self-service changes and reaccommodation without long support queues
Operational transparency when the journey is at risk
theirFear: Paying premium fares but still having to chase support during disruption
Fragmented servicing across airline, partner, and airport touchpoints
Reward value that feels hard to understand or hard to use

jobsToBeDone.name: Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Move from travel intent to a completed long-haul journey with clear value, trusted service, and low recovery friction when something changes.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher confidence to book direct, stronger repeat behaviour, and greater willingness to pay for premium travel moments.

steps.step: Find the right itinerary, fare, and premium extras
steps.description: Compare schedules, cabins, fare conditions, seats, and upgrade options without losing trust in the value proposition.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Offer, Pricing & Availability Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Builds the right flight and fare offers with schedule, fare-rule, and seat-availability context.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Ancillary, Seat & Upgrade Merchandising
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Presents seats, baggage, lounge, chauffeur, and upgrade decisions in ways that support premium conversion.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member JTBD step 1: Find the right itinerary, fare, and premium extras
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Find the right itinerary, fare, and premium extras.

steps.step: Keep identity, loyalty, and trip details joined up
steps.description: Carry profile, tier, points, payment preferences, and travel documents across booking and pre-travel servicing.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity, Profile & Document Readiness
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Connects traveller identity, passport and visa status, profile data, and contact preferences to the booking lifecycle.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Loyalty, Tier & Partner Rewards
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Makes points earning, redemption, tier benefits, and upgrade eligibility visible and actionable.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member JTBD step 2: Keep identity, loyalty, and trip details joined up
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Keep identity, loyalty, and trip details joined up.

steps.step: Stay informed and recover quickly when plans change
steps.description: Receive proactive alerts, understand options, and rebook or request help quickly when disruption occurs.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Trip Companion & Proactive Notifications
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps the guest informed about trip milestones, airport readiness, boarding, and changes in real time.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Disruption Recovery & Self-Service Reaccommodation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns schedule changes and cancellations into guided self-service recovery rather than manual call-centre dependency.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member JTBD step 3: Stay informed and recover quickly when plans change
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Stay informed and recover quickly when plans change.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member JTBD infographic: Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence" for customer "Global Frequent Flyer Member".

productStrategy.vision: Become the premium long-haul airline experience that travellers trust most for direct booking, loyalty value, and disruption recovery.

1_year.focus: Direct premium conversion and self-service servicing quality

businessKPI.northStar: Premium cabin revenue per available seat

milestones: Unified identity and loyalty recognition across booking and servicing
Premium offer and upgrade merchandising tuned to direct conversion
Self-service disruption recovery expanded to priority long-haul flows

3_year.focus: Personalised premium travel orchestration across own and partner journeys

supporting: Premium cabin revenue per available seat

milestones: Personalisation model across flight, loyalty, and partner inventory
Recognised traveller experience across alliance and airport handoffs
Trip recovery decisions increasingly automated by guest value and operational context

5_year.focus: Most trusted premium long-haul journey brand

supporting: Premium cabin revenue per available seat

milestones: Loyalty identity becomes the operating key for most premium journeys
Disruption recovery feels proactive and brand-differentiating rather than reactive
Partner and own journeys are serviced through one recognisable guest model

top.name: Trip confidence index
top.description: Composite measure of how confidently premium guests can shop, prepare, travel, and recover when plans change.

children.name: Premium booking conversion rate
children.description: Share of premium-intent shopping sessions that turn into confirmed bookings.

children.name: Trips linked to loyalty identity
children.description: Share of bookings and journeys connected to an identified loyalty profile.

children.name: Self-service disruption recovery rate
children.description: Share of eligible disrupted journeys resolved digitally without human intervention.

children.name: Pre-travel document readiness completion
children.description: Share of long-haul trips where required documents are confirmed before airport arrival.

top.name: Premium guest lifetime value
top.description: Expected contribution from a premium guest across repeat flights, ancillaries, loyalty, and partner spend.

children.name: Premium cabin revenue per available seat
children.description: Revenue generated from premium inventory relative to available capacity.

children.name: Direct digital booking mix
children.description: Share of bookings sold through owned digital channels.

children.name: Repeat guest rate
children.description: Share of premium guests who book again within the planning window.

customerJourneyStories.name: Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence
customerJourneyStories.summary: Global Frequent Flyer Member moves from recognizing that paying premium fares but still having to chase support during disruption to repeatedly achieving higher confidence to book direct, stronger repeat behaviour, and greater willingness to pay for premium travel moments through a journey that proves personalised offers and upgrades that respect status and trip context and protects finding the right premium itinerary without price or fare-rule confusion.

stages.narrative: The journey starts when Global Frequent Flyer Member sees that paying premium fares but still having to chase support during disruption is no longer acceptable, or when the current way of working can no longer protect finding the right premium itinerary without price or fare-rule confusion.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Trigger.

stages.narrative: Discovery happens when the offering shows up with proof points that connect directly to plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence and signal personalised offers and upgrades that respect status and trip context.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Discovery.

stages.narrative: Global Frequent Flyer Member evaluates whether the solution is relevant, easy to understand, and credible enough to improve finding the right premium itinerary without price or fare-rule confusion without creating fragmented servicing across airline, partner, and airport touchpoints.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: The trial succeeds when Global Frequent Flyer Member can quickly experience find the right itinerary, fare, and premium extras and keep identity, loyalty, and trip details joined up with limited friction and can see early evidence that move from travel intent to a completed long-haul journey with clear value, trusted service, and low recovery friction when something changes will work in the real environment.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Trial.

stages.narrative: Engagement deepens as Global Frequent Flyer Member uses the product to make stay informed and recover quickly when plans change part of normal operations, turning the initial promise into dependable day-to-day value anchored in one account that ties booking, loyalty, documents, seats, and service together.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Engagement.

stages.narrative: Retention comes when the product keeps making value visible through premium booking conversion rate, the product continues to make plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence easier and more dependable over time, and gives Global Frequent Flyer Member confidence that staying is safer than switching back or moving elsewhere.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence": Retention.

media.title: Global Frequent Flyer Member customer journey: Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence
media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Plan and take a premium long-haul trip with confidence" for customer "Global Frequent Flyer Member".

customers.name: Family Holiday Planner
customers.description: A leisure customer planning a higher-value holiday who wants flights, accommodation, extras, payments, and pre-travel preparation to feel like one joined-up purchase.
careAbout: Clarity on total holiday value, not just the airfare
Flexible payment and reassurance when booking early
Simple management of multiple passengers, documents, and extras
Confidence that support is available when family travel gets complex
winsThem: Holiday packages that are easy to compare, pay for, and amend
Clear visibility of what is included and what still needs action
Joined-up pre-travel communications for the whole booking
theirFear: A holiday package that becomes fragmented after purchase
Confusing payment deadlines, documentation gaps, or support handoffs
Having to manage flight and accommodation problems separately

jobsToBeDone.name: Plan and book a joined-up holiday without hidden complexity
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Bundle flights, hotel, extras, payment timing, and traveller preparation into one understandable holiday purchase and servicing flow.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher package conversion, lower support demand before departure, and stronger family travel trust.

steps.step: Compare complete holiday options and commit
steps.description: Evaluate package combinations, travel dates, room types, and holiday extras as one decision.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Holidays Packaging & Fulfilment
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Builds holiday packages that combine air, accommodation, transfers, and extras into bookable offers.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Offer, Pricing & Availability Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supplies accurate flight inventory and fare context inside the package.

media.title: Family Holiday Planner JTBD step 1: Compare complete holiday options and commit
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Plan and book a joined-up holiday without hidden complexity": Compare complete holiday options and commit.

steps.description: Manage deposits, balances, traveller details, seats, baggage, and document readiness without fragmented servicing.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Payments, Refunds & Revenue Settlement
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports deposits, staged balances, refunds, and partner settlements across the holiday lifecycle.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity, Profile & Document Readiness
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps passenger details, travel requirements, and contact information complete across the booking party.

media.title: Family Holiday Planner JTBD step 2: Coordinate payment, travellers, and documentation
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Plan and book a joined-up holiday without hidden complexity": Coordinate payment, travellers, and documentation.

steps.step: Get ready to travel with fewer surprises
steps.description: Receive guidance on what is booked, what still needs action, and how to get help before departure.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Trip Companion & Proactive Notifications
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Delivers clear pre-travel prompts, reminders, and trip-state visibility.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Customer Care & Case Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides agent-supported resolution for package-specific questions or exceptions.

media.title: Family Holiday Planner JTBD step 3: Get ready to travel with fewer surprises
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Plan and book a joined-up holiday without hidden complexity": Get ready to travel with fewer surprises.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Plan and book a joined-up holiday without hidden complexity" for customer "Family Holiday Planner".

productStrategy.vision: Make holidays feel like a differentiated, low-friction premium leisure product rather than a stitched-on booking add-on.

1_year.focus: Holiday package conversion and pre-travel servicing quality
milestones: Flights and holidays shopping and servicing model aligned
Staged payment and balance workflows simplified
Pre-travel readiness journey made visible for group bookings

3_year.focus: Premium leisure relationship and family lifetime value

revenueStreams: Cross-sell from holiday customers into loyalty and direct air travel

milestones: Family and leisure preferences drive offer personalisation
Holiday servicing covers most common amendments digitally
Holiday and loyalty economics become tightly connected

5_year.focus: Most trusted premium long-haul holiday brand
milestones: Household identity and holiday history shape package design
Most family package servicing is proactive and low-touch
Holiday demand becomes a core strategic complement to scheduled flying

top.description: Composite measure of how clearly and safely families can book, manage, and prepare for a holiday.

children.name: Holiday quote-to-book conversion
children.description: Share of holiday package quotes that convert to confirmed bookings.

children.name: Documentation completion rate
children.description: Share of holiday bookings with traveller information and required documents completed before departure.

children.name: Pre-travel support resolution time
children.description: Time needed to resolve package questions or issues before departure.

top.name: Holiday lifetime value per household
top.description: Expected contribution from a leisure household across repeat holiday and related travel purchases.

children.name: Holiday attachment revenue
children.description: Revenue attributable to holiday package products attached to flight demand.

children.name: Package gross margin
children.description: Gross margin generated from sold holiday packages.

children.name: Repeat holiday booking rate
children.description: Share of holiday bookers who purchase another holiday within the measurement window.

customers.name: Corporate Travel Program Manager
customers.description: A travel buyer or managed-travel leader responsible for policy-compliant, reliable long-haul travel for employees and executives.
careAbout: Access to the right negotiated content through preferred channels
Reliable disruption support for travelling employees
Clear reporting on adoption, policy compliance, and traveller pain
Trust that premium service and schedule quality justify the spend
winsThem: Consistent offer availability across agency, NDC, and direct channels
Fast support and recovery when travellers are disrupted
Program reporting that makes account value and problems visible
theirFear: Fragmented content and servicing across distribution channels
Travellers being stranded during disruption with poor support
Losing negotiated value because offer control is weak

jobsToBeDone.name: Manage a dependable, policy-compliant long-haul travel program
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Make it easy for travellers and agencies to book the right content while preserving support quality, policy compliance, and commercial visibility.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher corporate share of wallet, stronger traveller satisfaction, and fewer revenue leaks through unmanaged distribution.

steps.step: Distribute the right content to the right channels
steps.description: Expose negotiated fares, branded content, ancillaries, and servicing conditions consistently across agencies and program channels.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Corporate, Agency & NDC Distribution
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Controls commercial content, contract logic, and distribution pathways across managed-travel channels.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Offer, Pricing & Availability Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides the governed commercial offer layer behind distribution.

media.title: Corporate Travel Program Manager JTBD step 1: Distribute the right content to the right channels
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Manage a dependable, policy-compliant long-haul travel program": Distribute the right content to the right channels.

steps.step: Keep travellers moving when disruption happens
steps.description: Support rebooking, policy exceptions, and proactive recovery for travelling employees and executives.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Disruption Recovery & Self-Service Reaccommodation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Enables traveller-friendly recovery options and policy-aware exceptions.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Customer Care & Case Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports escalated or high-value servicing cases that require human coordination.

media.title: Corporate Travel Program Manager JTBD step 2: Keep travellers moving when disruption happens
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Manage a dependable, policy-compliant long-haul travel program": Keep travellers moving when disruption happens.

steps.step: Review value, compliance, and programme health
steps.description: Measure policy adherence, share of wallet, disruption burden, and account-level performance.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Operational Data, Revenue & Personalisation Platform
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Combines booking, revenue, and traveller-behaviour data into account-level insight.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Partner & Alliance Journey Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps partner-operated segments and onward journeys connected to the programme experience.

media.title: Corporate Travel Program Manager JTBD step 3: Review value, compliance, and programme health
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Manage a dependable, policy-compliant long-haul travel program": Review value, compliance, and programme health.

media.title: Corporate Travel Program Manager JTBD infographic: Manage a dependable, policy-compliant long-haul travel program
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Manage a dependable, policy-compliant long-haul travel program" for customer "Corporate Travel Program Manager".

productStrategy.vision: Become the preferred long-haul carrier for premium corporate travel by combining strong content control, dependable servicing, and superior recovery when schedules break.

1_year.focus: Content control and disruption credibility in managed travel
milestones: Negotiated content distributed with less fragmentation across channels
Corporate disruption recovery workflows tightened for priority travellers
Account-level visibility improved for programme and sales teams

3_year.focus: Commercial precision and recognised corporate traveller experiences
milestones: Corporate contract logic embedded in offer and servicing decisions
Priority traveller recognition extended deeper into airport and partner flows
Sales and programme teams work from one account-health model
Corporate servicing and disruption recovery feel materially better than commodity long-haul alternatives
Offer and distribution economics are optimized by account and channel
Priority traveller experience is recognisable across own and partner journeys

top.name: Corporate account satisfaction
top.description: Overall confidence of a managed-travel customer in the airline's content, servicing, and reliability.

children.name: In-policy booking rate
children.description: Share of corporate bookings made within program policy and preferred content paths.

children.name: Traveller disruption resolution time
children.description: Time needed to recover a disrupted corporate traveller into a viable itinerary.

children.name: Program reporting coverage
children.description: Share of account travel spend and incidents visible in reporting with enough detail to act on.

top.name: Corporate revenue share of wallet
top.description: Portion of a managed account's addressable long-haul demand captured by the airline and its controlled channels.

children.name: Corporate revenue growth
children.description: Year-over-year growth in managed corporate revenue.

children.name: NDC and direct business mix
children.description: Share of business bookings coming through controlled modern distribution pathways.

customers.name: Air Freight Forwarding Manager
customers.description: A freight-forwarding or cargo-operations leader who needs predictable capacity, milestone visibility, and exception handling for valuable or time-sensitive shipments.
careAbout: Fast capacity confirmation on constrained long-haul lanes
Digital booking and shipment visibility without manual chasing
Confidence on special handling, documentation, and exception escalation
Reliable service for premium cargo products such as pharma or perishables
winsThem: Digital quote, booking, and tracking with fewer handoffs
Operational transparency across milestones and exceptions
Predictable service levels on core trade lanes
theirFear: Calling or emailing repeatedly just to secure or update a shipment
Poor visibility after cargo has been accepted
High-value cargo being mishandled during disruption

jobsToBeDone.name: Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Secure capacity, tender the right shipment details, track movement, and resolve exceptions before they damage customer promises.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher digital cargo adoption, better premium-product mix, and stronger retention of freight customers on key routes.

steps.step: Get a quote and secure the right capacity
steps.description: Find the right service level, lane, and booking option quickly enough to win the shipment.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Cargo Capacity, Quote & Booking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides digital quoting, rate logic, service-level choice, and booking confirmation.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Partner & Alliance Journey Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Extends cargo reach through interline and partner capacity where appropriate.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager JTBD step 1: Get a quote and secure the right capacity
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Get a quote and secure the right capacity.

steps.step: Tender shipment details and special handling correctly
steps.description: Submit documentation, handling requirements, and operational details without manual rework.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Cargo Tracking & Exception Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Carries shipment milestones, handoff states, and exception markers through the cargo lifecycle.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Payments, Refunds & Revenue Settlement
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports cargo billing, adjustments, and settlement flows.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager JTBD step 2: Tender shipment details and special handling correctly
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Tender shipment details and special handling correctly.

steps.step: Monitor movement and resolve exceptions fast
steps.description: Stay ahead of delays, handling issues, or missed connections before the end consignee feels the failure.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Cargo Tracking & Exception Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Exposes milestone visibility and alerts for shipment progress and exceptions.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Customer Care & Case Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports high-touch exception resolution for premium or high-risk shipments.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager JTBD step 3: Monitor movement and resolve exceptions fast
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Monitor movement and resolve exceptions fast.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager JTBD infographic: Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility" for customer "Air Freight Forwarding Manager".

productStrategy.vision: Make cargo a digitally trusted premium extension of the long-haul network rather than a phone-and-email-led side business.

1_year.focus: Digital cargo booking adoption and exception transparency
milestones: Quote, booking, and tracking flows usable for more cargo cohorts
Premium cargo exceptions surfaced earlier and routed faster
Cargo digital experience aligned with network and handling realities

3_year.focus: Premium product differentiation and network-wide cargo visibility
milestones: Cargo service levels managed with more predictive exception handling
Partner and interline cargo flows integrated more deeply into digital visibility
Commercial and operations teams work from one cargo health model

5_year.focus: Best-in-class premium digital cargo experience on long-haul lanes
5_year.productTheme: Cargo commerce and operations control platform

revenueStreams: Lower servicing cost through digital adoption

milestones: Digital cargo becomes the default operating model for most shipments
Premium cargo exceptions are predicted and rerouted earlier
Cargo is treated as a strategic revenue product sharing the same control-tower foundations as passenger operations

top.name: Cargo flow confidence index
top.description: Composite measure of how confidently a freight customer can book, track, and recover a shipment.

children.name: Digital cargo booking share
children.description: Share of cargo bookings made through digital channels rather than manual channels.

children.name: Booking confirmation lead time
children.description: Time needed to return a confirmed booking decision for a shipment request.

children.name: Shipment milestone visibility coverage
children.description: Share of shipments with complete milestone visibility across the journey.

children.name: Exception resolution time
children.description: Time required to resolve an operational cargo exception to a customer-acceptable plan.

top.name: Cargo yield per available tonne
top.description: Revenue yield captured from available cargo capacity on the network.

children.name: Premium cargo product mix
children.description: Share of cargo revenue coming from higher-yield specialist products.

children.name: Freight customer retention
children.description: Share of strategic freight customers retained over the measurement period.

customerJourneyStories.name: Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility
customerJourneyStories.summary: Air Freight Forwarding Manager moves from recognizing that calling or emailing repeatedly just to secure or update a shipment to repeatedly achieving higher digital cargo adoption, better premium-product mix, and stronger retention of freight customers on key routes through a journey that proves digital quote, booking, and tracking with fewer handoffs and protects fast capacity confirmation on constrained long-haul lanes.

stages.narrative: The journey starts when Air Freight Forwarding Manager sees that calling or emailing repeatedly just to secure or update a shipment is no longer acceptable, or when the current way of working can no longer protect fast capacity confirmation on constrained long-haul lanes.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Trigger.

stages.narrative: Discovery happens when the offering shows up with proof points that connect directly to move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility and signal digital quote, booking, and tracking with fewer handoffs.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Discovery.

stages.narrative: Air Freight Forwarding Manager evaluates whether the solution is relevant, easy to understand, and credible enough to improve fast capacity confirmation on constrained long-haul lanes without creating poor visibility after cargo has been accepted.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: The trial succeeds when Air Freight Forwarding Manager can quickly experience get a quote and secure the right capacity and tender shipment details and special handling correctly with limited friction and can see early evidence that secure capacity, tender the right shipment details, track movement, and resolve exceptions before they damage customer promises will work in the real environment.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Trial.

stages.narrative: Engagement deepens as Air Freight Forwarding Manager uses the product to make monitor movement and resolve exceptions fast part of normal operations, turning the initial promise into dependable day-to-day value anchored in operational transparency across milestones and exceptions.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Engagement.

stages.narrative: Retention comes when the product keeps making value visible through digital cargo booking share, the product continues to make move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility easier and more dependable over time, and gives Air Freight Forwarding Manager confidence that staying is safer than switching back or moving elsewhere.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility": Retention.

media.title: Air Freight Forwarding Manager customer journey: Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility
media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Move time-sensitive cargo with predictable booking and visibility" for customer "Air Freight Forwarding Manager".

customers.name: Network Operations Control Director
customers.description: The leader responsible for day-of-operations decisions across aircraft, crew, airports, and guest recovery when the long-haul network is disrupted.
careAbout: Protecting completion factor and premium guest trust during irregular operations
Fast decisions across crew, aircraft, stations, and passenger recovery
Clear visibility of which flights, customers, and assets need intervention first
Lower compensation, hotel, and manual handling cost during disruption
winsThem: A control tower that joins operational risk, guest impact, and recovery options
Recovery tooling that treats passenger reaccommodation and operations decisions as one system
Reliable airport, baggage, and customer-service handoffs
theirFear: Solving aircraft or crew problems while creating unmanageable guest fallout
Fragmented disruption tools that force manual coordination across teams
Premium brand damage caused by weak communication and recovery

jobsToBeDone.name: Run a resilient long-haul operation through disruption
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Detect operational risk early, make recovery tradeoffs across constrained assets, and keep guest fallout visible and manageable.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher completion factor, faster recovery, and lower guest compensation and handling cost during irregular operations.

steps.step: See network risk and guest impact early
steps.description: Understand which flights, rotations, stations, and guest cohorts are most at risk before the disruption cascades.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Operational Control Tower
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Presents network health, station risk, and disruption priorities in one operating view.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Operational Data, Revenue & Personalisation Platform
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Combines operational, customer, and commercial data to rank recovery options by impact.

media.title: Network Operations Control Director JTBD step 1: See network risk and guest impact early
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Run a resilient long-haul operation through disruption": See network risk and guest impact early.

steps.step: Recover aircraft, crew, and guest itineraries together
steps.description: Sequence operational recovery in a way that protects both the schedule and the most valuable guest journeys.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Crew & Aircraft Recovery Optimization
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports network tradeoffs for aircraft swaps, crew legality, and rotation repair.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Disruption Recovery & Self-Service Reaccommodation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns operational decisions into actionable options for guests and service teams.

media.title: Network Operations Control Director JTBD step 2: Recover aircraft, crew, and guest itineraries together
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Run a resilient long-haul operation through disruption": Recover aircraft, crew, and guest itineraries together.

steps.step: Close the loop across airport, baggage, and care teams
steps.description: Coordinate airport handling, baggage exceptions, customer care, and after-action learning.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Airport, Baggage & Ground Journey Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Coordinates airport and baggage states that often determine whether recovery actually works on the ground.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Customer Care & Case Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Routes high-priority guest exceptions to the right support paths with context from the disruption.

media.title: Network Operations Control Director JTBD step 3: Close the loop across airport, baggage, and care teams
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Run a resilient long-haul operation through disruption": Close the loop across airport, baggage, and care teams.

media.title: Network Operations Control Director JTBD infographic: Run a resilient long-haul operation through disruption
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Run a resilient long-haul operation through disruption" for customer "Network Operations Control Director".

productStrategy.vision: Run a premium long-haul airline where operational recovery decisions and guest recovery decisions are managed as one connected system.

1_year.focus: Operational stability and guest-aware disruption recovery
1_year.productTheme: Integrated operations and guest recovery control

businessKPI.northStar: Irregular operations cost per disrupted flight

milestones: Control-tower view aligns operational risk with guest impact
Passenger reaccommodation and flight recovery workflows linked more tightly
Airport and care teams work from shared disruption context

3_year.focus: Predictive recovery and premium-service protection during disruption

supporting: Irregular operations cost per disrupted flight

milestones: Predictive disruption alerts trigger earlier recovery plays
Priority guest and connection logic influences recovery decisions
Most common disruption cases resolve through repeatable digital playbooks

businessKPI.northStar: Irregular operations cost per disrupted flight

milestones: Recovery decisions are optimized across network, revenue, and guest impact in near real time
Airport and digital guest recovery become two views of the same operating workflow
The airline is known for handling disruption materially better than competitors

top.name: Completion factor
top.description: Share of planned flights operated to completion.

children.name: Recovery decision cycle time
children.description: Time needed to move from disruption detection to an agreed recovery action.

children.name: Misconnected guest rate
children.description: Share of guests whose onward journey fails due to missed or broken connections.

children.name: Baggage exception closure time
children.description: Time needed to close baggage-related exceptions caused by disruption.

top.name: Irregular operations cost per disrupted flight
top.description: Direct operational and guest-handling cost incurred for each materially disrupted flight.

children.name: On-time departure rate
children.description: Share of flights departing within the target on-time threshold.

children.name: Guest compensation cost
children.description: Compensation, hotel, meal, and recovery cost attributable to guest disruption handling.
